questions.question: What are the two layers that make up the dcipher network architecture?
Smart contracts in dcipher perform the actual threshold cryptography computations on-chain.
How does dcipher prevent a single operator from biasing the randomness output?
options: By using block hashes as the source of randomness
By requiring operators to commit to their random contributions before revealing them
By generating randomness on a centralized server
By using VRF (Verifiable Random Function) from a single trusted node
questions.correctAnswer: By requiring operators to commit to their random contributions before revealing them

questions.question: In Blocklock, the decryption key doesn't exist before the condition is met—it's generated ______ by the committee.
What type of encryption does Blocklock use where the condition itself becomes the identity?
If you compromise one operator's key share in a threshold system, you can decrypt all Blocklock-encrypted data.
Why does dcipher use BLS signatures on the BN254 curve?
options: They are faster to compute than other signature schemes
They are aggregatable, allowing multiple signatures to combine into one compact signature
questions.correctAnswer: They are aggregatable, allowing multiple signatures to combine into one compact signature
